competitors.name: The AI Exchange / Rachel Woods
competitors.category: AI operations education, playbooks, operator certification
competitors.audience: operators, consultants, team leads, business operators
verified_facts: Official site positions the offer as Certified AI Operator for operators, consultants and team leads.
Official site says learners run AI Operations cycles, architect systems and lead AI adoption using the CRAFT framework.
Certification is listed as starting at $1,800 on the official site.
Rachel Woods also runs AMP, a free AI operations newsletter for leaders.
competitors.lead_magnet: Free AI operations newsletter / playbook-driven content.
competitors.paid_offer: Certified AI Operator; exact current membership pricing was not confirmed in this check, so do not hardcode $49.99/month unless re-verified.
what_to_study_for_kora: Language of AI Operations, playbooks, cycles, adoption, systems.
How to sell AI implementation as an operating capability, not as tool training.
Certification/community framing for future KORA AI Operator style product.
competitors.kora_adaptation: Use the AI Operations idea but translate it to small-business language: processes, заявки, контент, клиентские ответы, CRM, Notion, Telegram/WhatsApp.
competitors.avoid_copying: Do not position KORA as enterprise certification before there is a real curriculum, assessment and certificate process.

competitors.name: Skill Leap by Futurepedia
competitors.category: AI education platform / course library / membership
verified_facts: Official site offers Free Starter access at $0/forever with two full courses and no credit card required.
Official site lists Monthly All Access at $29.99/mo, 30+ premium courses, 1,000+ videos, prompt vault, guides and community.
Futurepedia courses page describes 1,000+ lessons across 30+ courses.
competitors.lead_magnet: Free Starter access / free trial / course samples.
competitors.paid_offer: $29.99/mo Monthly All Access listed on the official site at the time of review.
what_to_study_for_kora: Membership structure: library + prompt vault + community + weekly content.
Learning paths by job-to-be-done, not by tool name only.
How to make AI education feel practical and not academic.
competitors.kora_adaptation: Future product: KORA AI Library with Russian mini-lessons, prompt packs, business workflows and templates.
competitors.avoid_copying: Do not launch a full subscription library until there is enough content depth; start with a lead magnet and low-ticket mini-product.

competitors.name: Liam Ottley / Morningside AI
competitors.audience: companies needing AI automation; people building AI agencies
verified_facts: Morningside AI states that it helps companies identify AI opportunities, then build, deploy and train teams to use them.
Liam Ottley's personal site links to a free community, Morningside AI and AAA Accelerator.
competitors.paid_offer: Agency services and accelerator; exact pricing not public in reviewed official snippets.
what_to_study_for_kora: Agency + education flywheel: content -> community -> consulting/implementation.
Language of identifying AI opportunities before building.
Case-led implementation positioning.
competitors.kora_adaptation: Use the build/deploy/train sequence for KORA’s implementation offer: диагностика -> сценарий -> настройка -> обучение.
competitors.avoid_copying: Avoid aggressive 'start an AI agency' business-opportunity framing; KORA is calmer and service-business oriented.

competitors.name: Allie K. Miller
competitors.category: AI personal brand, advisory, speaking, education
competitors.audience: founders, executives, professionals, AI-curious business audience
verified_facts: Official site presents Allie as an AI business leader and international speaker.
Official site offers a free 5-day AI Fast Track email course.
Official site includes services, courses, resources and newsletter.
competitors.lead_magnet: Free 5-day AI Fast Track email course.
competitors.paid_offer: Advisory, speaking, courses/products; exact prices not hardcoded here.
what_to_study_for_kora: Founder-led trust architecture.
Email course as a low-friction lead magnet.
Executive-friendly language: tactics, tools, business outcomes.
competitors.kora_adaptation: Create KORA 5-day email/Telegram sequence: 'AI без хаоса: где начать внедрение в бизнесе'.
competitors.avoid_copying: Do not overinflate founder bio; use real background and practical business experience only.

competitors.name: The Rundown AI / Rowan Cheung
competitors.category: AI media, newsletter, guides, tools, AI university
competitors.audience: broad AI audience: professionals, founders, operators, creators
verified_facts: Official site positions the newsletter as learning AI in 5 minutes a day.
Official site claims 2,000,000+ readers and includes articles, podcast, guides, tools, workshops and AI University.
Rundown AI University page lists 100+ guides, expert-led workshops and Rundown Pro at $84/month billed annually.
competitors.lead_magnet: Free daily newsletter and guides ecosystem.
what_to_study_for_kora: Newsletter-first distribution system.
Short practical guides and tool explanations.
How to bridge free content, guides and paid education.
competitors.kora_adaptation: Create a Russian-language 'AI без хаоса' content engine: one task, one scenario, one tool, one mistake to avoid.
competitors.avoid_copying: Do not try to become mass AI news media; KORA should stay implementation-focused and service-led.

competitors.name: Future Tools / Matt Wolfe
competitors.category: AI tools database + AI news + newsletter
competitors.audience: AI tool seekers, creators, founders, operators
verified_facts: Official site has Tools, AI News, Videos, Glossary, Newsletter, FAQ and Submit a Tool sections.
Search result from official site states a twice-weekly AI briefing and 230,000+ readers.
competitors.paid_offer: Sponsorship/media ecosystem; exact monetization not hardcoded here.
what_to_study_for_kora: Tool categorization by use case and pricing type.
Simple discovery UX for 'which tool fits my problem'.
Tool submission/filtering patterns for future directory.
competitors.kora_adaptation: Build a small curated 'KORA tool map' later: tools by business task, not a huge uncontrolled directory.
competitors.avoid_copying: Do not build a giant AI tool catalog now; it will distract from services and rot quickly.

competitors.name: No Code MBA
verified_facts: Official courses page lists 11 project-based courses on the modern AI stack.
Official page says all courses are one membership from $29.99/month.
Skills listed include Cursor, Claude, Lovable, Replit, ChatGPT, n8n, Bubble, Supabase, Airtable, Webflow, Zapier and Make.
competitors.paid_offer: Course membership from $29.99/month on official courses page.
what_to_study_for_kora: Project-based promise: build a real output, not just learn theory.
Tool-stack clarity for non-technical users.
Membership as access to all courses.
competitors.kora_adaptation: KORA workshops should end with tangible outputs: first bot flow, first prompt library, first content workflow, first CRM automation map.
competitors.avoid_copying: Do not sell 'build a SaaS' if KORA’s core audience wants business process help, not startup building.

competitors.name: Everyday AI / Jordan Wilson
competitors.category: Daily AI podcast/newsletter + courses by business track
competitors.audience: professionals, business owners, marketers, sales, startups
verified_facts: Official course page has a free ChatGPT 101 course with 12 lessons.
Navigation includes AI tracks such as AI for Content Creation, AI for Marketing, AI for Sales, AI for Startups and Businesses.
Official site/podcast positions Everyday AI as podcast/newsletter.
competitors.paid_offer: Courses, consulting/training, partnerships; exact public pricing not hardcoded here.
what_to_study_for_kora: AI tracks by business function.
Daily simple content cadence.
Free course as trust-building entry.
competitors.kora_adaptation: Group KORA education by tasks: AI для заявок, AI для контента, AI для клиентских вопросов, AI для команды, AI для базы знаний.
competitors.avoid_copying: Do not become a general daily AI news outlet; keep every topic tied to implementation.

competitors.name: Ben’s Bites / Ben Tossell
competitors.category: AI builder newsletter, tutorials, tool reviews, community
competitors.audience: AI builders, founders, technically curious non-engineers
verified_facts: Official Substack says it is a newsletter about startups and investing for AI builders of all levels.
Official site says it has over 168,000 subscribers.
About page lists vetted Tuesday/Thursday summaries, videos, insights/how-tos, founder/company commentary and community access; community access is paid and costs $80/year at time of review.
competitors.lead_magnet: Newsletter with mix of free and paid posts.
competitors.paid_offer: $80/year community access on official About page at time of review.
what_to_study_for_kora: Founder voice + practical experiments + tool reviews.
Paid community with low annual price and low tier complexity.
Useful tutorials over polished theory.
competitors.kora_adaptation: Use a founder-led Telegram/email format with tested workflows and business use cases; later add paid community/library.
competitors.avoid_copying: Do not over-index on builders/startups if KORA’s immediate buyer is a business owner or expert needing implementation.

competitors.name: Jeff Su
competitors.audience: professionals, Google Workspace users, productivity audience
verified_facts: Official newsletter page offers one practical Google Workspace and Gemini tip every week.
The newsletter is framed as a 1% weekly productivity improvement and lists 46,740 readers at review time.
Official courses page promotes a Notion Command Center system/course with database templates and walkthroughs.
competitors.lead_magnet: Newsletter, free toolkit/email course, YouTube content.
competitors.paid_offer: Courses, templates, toolkits; exact current pricing not hardcoded here.
what_to_study_for_kora: Template/workflow monetization.
App-specific practical tips.
Productivity framing without AI hype.
competitors.kora_adaptation: Create KORA templates: AI audit sheet, prompt vault, client FAQ builder, content repurposing matrix, Notion/CRM handoff checklist.
competitors.avoid_copying: Do not narrow KORA to productivity hacks; keep the implementation/automation offer central.
